old_claim.note: Human anatomy and physiology was treated as an enabling capability for iatrochemistry without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct anatomy-and-physiology dependency remains because the node is specifically Paracelsian chemical medicine, not Vesalian anatomical study as the required input.

source_shape.source_locator: Overview and history sections: the article links iatrochemistry to chemical explanations of disease and Paracelsus rather than to anatomical dissection or Vesalian physiology.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports chemical explanation and remedy-making as the defining iatrochemical relationship.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Anatomy and physiology are neighboring medical sciences; making them direct prerequisites would blur a chemical-medicine node with anatomical reform.

ontology_before: Iatrochemistry depended directly on Human Anatomy and Physiology, implying anatomical reform was required for the Paracelsian chemical-medicine node.
ontology_after: Iatrochemistry keeps Alchemy and Scientific Method as direct prerequisites and removes the anatomy edge as broad medical context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: human_anatomy_physiology is no longer a direct prerequisite for iatrochemistry_paracelsus.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to physiological chemistry rather than Paracelsian iatrochemistry.
A stronger source showed human anatomy and physiology directly enabled the represented iatrochemical movement.
A dedicated medical_reform_synthesis node replaced this one and intentionally combined chemical and anatomical reform.
short_reason: Anatomy is neighboring medical reform, not the direct iatrochemical anchor.
